findings.description: Critical: Task 5.5 (repo-wide stale-reference sweep) is defined in tasks.md but remains unassigned to any work package in work-packages.yaml, and no work package has the write-scope for repo-root prose files (CLAUDE.md, README.md, docs/, apps/). This leaves the stale import in .github/workflows/ci.yml (Codex round-2 finding #3) unaddressed by any active agent.
findings.resolution: Assign Task 5.5 to wp-integration, update its description to include 'Covers tasks 5.5, 7.1-7.6', and expand its write_allow scope to include the root CLAUDE.md, README.md, docs/**, apps/**, and .github/**.

findings.description: Critical: The verification step for wp-skills-update still only greps for the dotted form evaluation.gen_eval, failing to verify that slash-path references like the validate-feature descriptor-discovery glob have been updated. This ignores the round-2 nit from Codex finding #4 and contradicts the corrected checkpoint 5.C in tasks.md.
findings.resolution: Update the wp-skills-update verification command to check for both 'evaluation.gen_eval' and 'evaluation/gen_eval' as specified in Task 5.C.

findings.description: Nit: The description for wp-coordinator-migrate still claims to update the .github/workflows/ci.yml coordinator docker-build step, but later correctly states this task is owned by wp-integration. The package also lacks the write_allow scope for the CI file, making the claim inaccurate.
findings.resolution: Remove the claim about updating ci.yml from the wp-coordinator-migrate description (lines 268-270) to align with its actual scope and Task 4.5(b).

findings.description: The adoption of Strategy A (repo-root build context) is consistently reflected across D8, Task 4.5, and Task 7.6. The plan correctly eliminates the complex wheel prebuild and --no-sources juggling from Option B, and the wp-integration verification step is strong enough to confirm the CI repoint matches the new Dockerfile context.
